answers.object: terminal trace widget for lane monitoring
answers.goal: let operators tell quickly when a lane is drifting

answers.question: should the web app lead with linked projects or the old idea board?
answers.chosen_path: lead with linked projects first.
answers.rationale: active work should be foregrounded and idea browsing can move into Pensieve.
answers.consequences: the old idea board becomes secondary navigation.

answers.claim: short drift summaries will help operators notice stalled lanes faster.
answers.assumptions: operators glance at summaries while they work.
answers.test_path: compare stalled-lane pickup with and without summaries.

answers.objective: compare free-form tasks, checklist tasks, and kernel tasks during handoff pickup
answers.method: give matched task artifacts to a second operator and time correct interpretation

answers.completed_unit: restored linked-project routing for Habanero
answers.current_state: the repo is bound and the primary session is routable again
answers.risks: other machines may still need a sync

answers.rule: only claim hosted jobs for linked projects that have a routeable local session.
answers.rationale: avoid claiming work with nowhere real to execute.
answers.invariants: a claimed job must resolve to an actual local session.

answers.claim: ORP shipped the first reasoning kernel release.
answers.next_follow_up: run comparative studies against free-form artifacts and checklist artifacts.

answers.object: the terminal trace widget for lane monitoring
answers.goal: Build the terminal trace widget for lane monitoring.
answers.boundary: Terminal-first lane visibility in active ORP sessions.
answers.success_criteria: An operator can identify a drifting lane quickly.

answers.chosen_path: Lead with linked projects and move broader idea browsing into Pensieve.
answers.rejected_alternatives: Alternative considered: keep the idea board as the front door.
answers.consequences: Idea discovery becomes less prominent on the home screen.

answers.claim: Drift summaries will improve stalled-lane pickup speed.
answers.assumptions: Assumes operators actually consult the summary lane.
answers.test_path: Add summaries and compare pickup behavior against a no-summary baseline.

answers.objective: Compare handoff pickup across free-form, checklist, and kernel task artifacts.
answers.method: Give a second operator one artifact at a time and record time to correct interpretation.

answers.completed_unit: Restored the Habanero linked-project routing path.
answers.current_state: The local repo, primary session, and hosted world are aligned again.
answers.risks: Other active machines may still carry stale routing state.
answers.next_handoff_target: Rerun runner sync on active machines and verify live pickup.

answers.rule: Only claim hosted jobs for linked projects that have a routeable local session.
answers.rationale: The rule exists to prevent dead-end job claims.
answers.invariants: do not claim unroutable jobs

answers.claim: ORP shipped the first reasoning kernel release in the CLI.
answers.next_follow_up: Run comparative artifact and handoff studies next.

answers.object: terminal trace widget
answers.goal: surface lane drift and state clearly for operators
answers.success_criteria: ["an operator can identify a drifting lane within 10 seconds","the widget does not overload the terminal surface"]

answers.question: Should the web app home foreground linked projects or the old idea board?
answers.chosen_path: Foreground linked projects and move broad idea browsing into Pensieve.
answers.rejected_alternatives: ["keep the old idea board as the default home","split the home evenly between ideas and projects"]
answers.rationale: Active work should be reachable immediately, while the idea library can stay available as secondary navigation.
answers.consequences: ["linked projects become the primary home object","idea browsing becomes one click deeper"]

answers.claim: Short drift summaries reduce the time needed to identify stalled lanes.
answers.boundary: ["terminal-first multi-lane workflows","operators already monitoring active lanes"]
answers.assumptions: ["operators glance at summaries while they work","summaries do not introduce excessive noise"]
answers.test_path: Run matched stalled-lane pickup trials with and without summaries and compare detection time.
answers.falsifiers: ["pickup time does not improve materially","operators ignore the summaries"]

answers.objective: Measure whether kernel task artifacts improve handoff pickup quality over free-form and generic checklist alternatives.
answers.method: Run matched handoff trials where a second operator receives one artifact at a time and explains the task, constraints, and next action.
answers.interpretation_limits: ["internal sample size is small","the pilot measures structural pickup, not full downstream outcomes"]

answers.completed_unit: Restored canonical runner routing for Habanero.
answers.current_state: The local project link, primary session, and hosted world are synchronized and routable again.
answers.risks: ["inactive machines may still hold stale routing state","older queued jobs may need a fresh sync before pickup"]
answers.next_handoff_target: Rerun runner sync on active machines and verify one fresh hosted job pickup.

answers.scope: Hosted runner job pickup and claim behavior.
answers.rule: Only claim a hosted job when the linked project has a routeable local session on the current machine.
answers.rationale: Jobs should only be claimed when the runner can execute them against a real local target.
answers.invariants: ["a claimed job must resolve to an actual local session","runner routing must stay machine-scoped"]
answers.enforcement_surface: runner sync, poll, and work lifecycle

answers.claim: ORP shipped the first reasoning kernel release as a real CLI protocol surface.
answers.status: shipped in the ORP CLI and published to npm
answers.interpretation_limits: ["comparative superiority over free-form and checklist alternatives is not yet proven","the current evidence is strongest on internal validity"]
answers.next_follow_up: Run the comparative artifact, pickup, and corpus-fit studies.

answers.goal: surface lane drift and state clearly for operators
answers.success_criteria: ["an operator can identify a drifting lane within 10 seconds","the widget does not overload the terminal surface"]

answers.question: Should the web app home foreground linked projects or the old idea board?
answers.chosen_path: Foreground linked projects and move broad idea browsing into Pensieve.
answers.rejected_alternatives: ["keep the old idea board as the default home","split the home evenly between ideas and projects"]
answers.rationale: Active work should be reachable immediately, while the idea library can stay available as secondary navigation.
answers.consequences: ["linked projects become the primary home object","idea browsing becomes one click deeper"]

answers.claim: Short drift summaries reduce the time needed to identify stalled lanes.
answers.boundary: ["terminal-first multi-lane workflows","operators already monitoring active lanes"]
answers.assumptions: ["operators glance at summaries while they work","summaries do not introduce excessive noise"]
answers.test_path: Run matched stalled-lane pickup trials with and without summaries and compare detection time.
answers.falsifiers: ["pickup time does not improve materially","operators ignore the summaries"]

answers.objective: Measure whether kernel task artifacts improve handoff pickup quality over free-form and generic checklist alternatives.
answers.method: Run matched handoff trials where a second operator receives one artifact at a time and explains the task, constraints, and next action.
answers.interpretation_limits: ["internal sample size is small","the pilot measures structural pickup, not full downstream outcomes"]

answers.completed_unit: Restored canonical runner routing for Habanero.
answers.current_state: The local project link, primary session, and hosted world are synchronized and routable again.
answers.risks: ["inactive machines may still hold stale routing state","older queued jobs may need a fresh sync before pickup"]
answers.next_handoff_target: Rerun runner sync on active machines and verify one fresh hosted job pickup.

answers.scope: Hosted runner job pickup and claim behavior.
answers.rule: Only claim a hosted job when the linked project has a routeable local session on the current machine.
answers.rationale: Jobs should only be claimed when the runner can execute them against a real local target.
answers.invariants: ["a claimed job must resolve to an actual local session","runner routing must stay machine-scoped"]
answers.enforcement_surface: runner sync, poll, and work lifecycle

answers.claim: ORP shipped the first reasoning kernel release as a real CLI protocol surface.
answers.status: shipped in the ORP CLI and published to npm
answers.interpretation_limits: ["comparative superiority over free-form and checklist alternatives is not yet proven","the current evidence is strongest on internal validity"]
answers.next_follow_up: Run the comparative artifact, pickup, and corpus-fit studies.
